title: Conflict and Humanitarian Crises: Research & Analysis | CSIS
gist: This CSIS topic-landing page for Conflict and Humanitarian Crises is a directory of newsletters, podcasts and events rather than a single article; its item descriptions flag a US-Iran framework deal with a 60-day ceasefire and Hormuz reopening, Iran's war-driven economic and food crisis, the CRINK axis, and the Vatican outmaneuvering Washington on AI policy.

insights.claim: CSIS reports the US and Iran announced a framework deal featuring a 60-day ceasefire and the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz.
insights.speaker: CSIS
insights.so_what: A Hormuz reopening and ceasefire would relieve oil/LNG chokepoint risk and energy price pressure; watch durability of the 60-day window.
insights.quote: U.S. and Iran announce a framework deal with a 60‑day ceasefire and reopening of the Strait of Hormuz.
insights.locator: Trump's Iran Deal: What Comes Next?

insights.claim: A CSIS newsletter states the war has exacerbated Iran's economic collapse and its cost-of-living crisis, including a food crisis.
insights.speaker: CSIS
insights.so_what: Domestic Iranian economic distress raises regime-stability and regional-spillover risk to monitor.
insights.quote: The war has exacerbated Iran's economic collapse and its cost-of-living crisis.

insights.claim: CSIS frames a question of how the United States should counter the CRINK axis, indicating an analytical focus on the China-Russia-Iran-North Korea grouping.
insights.speaker: CSIS
insights.so_what: Signals consolidation of an adversary-bloc framing relevant to defense and sanctions positioning.
insights.quote: explore strategies to counter the CRINK axis.
insights.locator: How Should the United States Counter the CRINK Axis?

insights.claim: A CSIS commentary by Benjamin Jensen argues the Vatican's AI intervention exposes Washington's blind spot of debating hypothetical doomsday AGI scenarios while missing real crises reshaping human dignity, work, truth and power.
insights.speaker: CSIS
insights.so_what: Reframes AI-policy debate toward near-term societal harms over speculative AGI risk; relevant to AI governance direction.
insights.quote: Washington must move beyond abstract AGI fears and build a civil society dialogue on human dignity, supply chains, truth, and statecraft.
insights.locator: The Vatican Just Outmaneuvered Washington on AI
